title: crepis runcinata
intro: Crepis runcinata, also known as the rough hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Crepis runcinata include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and boosting the immune system, as well as being used as a natural remedy for fever and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
